processes.process:
1. Create WO based on customer request and part availability
2. Select delivery option (2h/4h/NBD)
3. If part unavailable locally, fallback to NBD
4. Escalate OOH via L3 contact if needed
5. If Severity 1, create PER after releasing the SBD WO

1. Exclude Morabanc and Andbanc (handled by Spain)
2. Use ship to customer = Yes
3. Only NBD delivery (2-3 days expected)

1. BBU not replaced, customer purchases separately
2. CMOS treated as CRU, it's considered a consumable part - expected delivery date +2J - If the battery is not delivered correctly due to customer refused the part, we will not place a second order. Ask the customer to obtain the part - According to Regis Lenovo SDM (never approach him for exception).
3. If customer insisted to have SSR intervention, Request approval from Lenovo SDM via the OVD process - if accepted the CMOS should not be delivered in Access point.
4. Lenovo SDM will determine whether an ONS fee applies (one case, one fee per SN) or if hourly billing should be used (in case of high volumes of SN).
5. Proof of approval via email and the OVD file.

1. Require OL approval
2. Cancel duplicates, test cases, incorrect cases
3. Cancel not entitled or declined T&M cases
4. review golden rule document if necessary.

1. Contact customer at least 3 times
2. Send closure notification
3. Close within 7 days if no response
4. Record all actions in system

1. any case created under a country different from the server’s actual location must be canceled and re-created with the correct country.
2. This is not limited to Drom-Com regions.
3. Ensure the Metrics tab is checked to verify the country originally associated with the case:

1. Basically they have active Growth contract seen in warranty look-up
2. If yes, proceed to handle on CSP, use Ecurep for communication, cst may send logs to LSC or attached to mail.
3. If escalation to L3 (ATS) is required, create MSD case.
4. If part replacement is required, create MSD if there wasn't any, order part ONS (ship to customer - NO).
5. After releasing the MSD WO, cancel the labor onsite service, and make sure the newly generated TS is cancelled.
6. On the original TS (for Growth contract) make sure to share with CC/SSR the ETA of part delivery and all necessary information.
7. Make sure not to request FW update unless L3 insist on it , customer should have best recipe.
8. Customer must confirm closure

1. Ignore postal codes starting with 98xxx (except Monaco 98000)
2. Handle 97xxx with SSR only
3. No CRU, ship to customer = No

1. Use MSD escalation template
2. Include logs, system state, actions taken
3. Define business impact and expected resolution time

1. Verify contract status in system
2. If no contract found, send email to customer requesting valid contract or offer quotation
3. If contract provided, send to SDM for approval
4. If quotation requested by customer, review T&M Process (https://w3.ibm.com/w3publisher/lenovo-isg/process-documents/t-m-process)
5. If signed, proceed with case handling (create WO, update entitlement)
6. Signed order confirmations now should be send to our Functional ID (Lenovo.ISG.EMEA.TLS.Delivery@ibm.com) instead of market CSMs - 
7. When RTS are handling the tickets when dispatching to field, they have to add in the action plan the respective case is billable and SSRs have to bring an on-site voucher with them.
8. SSRs when completing the voucher they have to send it towards the Functional ID instead of market CSMs like in the past.
9. If not signed or no response in 5 days, cancel case.

Special handling suppose to stop end of December 2026 upon contracts renewal:

Proposed process:
•	All Reseller mode machines are registered in Conga under the IBM Growth contract for CSP entitlement (for cost transfer).
•	The client opens cases in CSP.
•	The IBM Client team (Philippe - philippe_thibaut@fr.ibm.com) monitors the cases in CSP to ensure the IBM RTS team identifies them as Forvia cases.
•	The IBM RTS team verifies the serial numbers against Forvia list of SNs - https://ibm-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/personal/nlabib_eg_ibm_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7BB46795E1-3664-4A15-A50E-D906B83E228E%7D&file=Copy%20of%20Forvia%20(Faurecia)%20Datacenter%20Lenovo%20inv%202026.xlsx&action=default&mobileredirect=true
•	The IBM RTS also make sure on Lenovo Waranty lookup that contract is not expired or Limited (IOL).
•	The IBM RTS team opens MSD case and keeps CPS case open.
•	The IBM RTS team updates both CSP and MSD cases (all communications should be on the TS case).
•	If SSR onsite support is required, a new TS case is opened (B2B MSD/CSP)
o	2nd TS case will be reference in the 1st TS case to inform the client (make sure Customer/CC team are aware of the new TS ref#).
o	2nd TS case will become main TS to communicate with the client about onsite scheduling etc (by CC team).

1. It is very important that everyone is fully aware of the required escalation logs needed for GPU related cases across different system types.
2. IBM RTS L1 are required to collect the list of logs detailed below during the first client contact, prior to any escalation of the case, driving a reduced time for analysis from PE/Dev teams and for improving the quality of analysis outcome for the client.. 

DCGMI diagnostics OS logs. 
nvidia-bug-report 
nvidia-smi -q 
OS log

Here is a link to the GLOSSE site for details
https://glosse4lenovo.lenovo.com/wiki/glosse4lenovo/view/Logs/?srid=HknbLrn1#HGPUsystems

3. Please set customer expectation if PE agrees to replace the GPU there will be some time until part delivery. 
4. Case should be escalated to L3 , Must be approved by PE before replacement.

Please take in consideration the following:

A new diagnostic approach being applied in certain GPU-related cases.
Lenovo has introduced the use of an NVIDIA Partner Diagnostic (also referred to as FieldDiag / GPU Service Toolkit – GST), which is an NDA-controlled tool used by SSR engineers onsite to validate GPU failures prior to replacement.
> Key points:
•	This tool is used to confirm GPU hardware failure before proceeding with replacement
•	It runs advanced diagnostics (Field Diag, NVDebug, DCGM tests)
•	The generated logs are reviewed by ATS / L3 before further action
> Please note:
•	This tool is strictly NDA-controlled.
•	Each GPU/platform may require a different version of the tool, provided case-by-case
•	This is not a reusable tool for general troubleshooting
•	The execution of the tool is the customer’s responsibility
•	It is not performed by IBM SSR anymore.

1. Whenever you receive a case in Mahi that is identified as a Growth contract, the case must be canceled.
2. A new case must be opened in CSP instead.
3. No RTS time should be reported in Mahi for Growth contract cases unless it's open for escalation to L3 or for part ordering.
4. Parts should be ordered on MSD as CRU unless there is a special handling associated to specific reason.
5. Please note that the customer is already aware that they have a Growth contract and is required to log cases through the IBM portal, not the Lenovo portal. If needed, you may educate and reinforce this process with the customer.
6. All RTS time must be reported accurately in CSP

* customer: Université de Montpellier
* All Machines are in HPC cluster solution.
* All MSD case should be opened or corrected with the HPC Serial number not the node.

1.	Always communicate by email (unless customer requests phone communication)
2.	Always keep email history in communication
3.	If power / temperature issue, always check with customer if they encountered any issue with CDU (if case needs L3 assistance, always inform L3 of CDU installation)
4.	Never request customer to confirm asset location. All machines are hosted in CINES datacenter (CINES - 950 rue St Priest - 34090 Montpellier – France) Local contact : Jean-Luc OMS - 06 32 01 04 17
5.	SSR needs to have ZRR accreditation (RMC has approved list)

1. WO should be ship to customer “YES” NBD
2. In the WO change the address of original customer to be (IS2 MAYOTTE - ZI de Kaweni Immeuble somibat - 97600 MAMOUDZOU - MAYOTTE)
3. Make sure in the WO instructions to clarify the original server location for the SSR to be aware, because in CSP WO address will be the one of IS2 where part will be delivered.

Lenovo ISG has introduced a dedicated functional ID for requests related to missing return labels, including SOIDs and UPS return labels.
Please send all such requests to:
📧 isgrtvreturn@lenovo.com

1. Location

Site: Interxion PAR3 (PARIS ITX)
Address: 7–9 Avenue des Arts et Métiers, 93200 Saint-Denis, France 


2. Work Order & Parts Handling

Create Labor-only WO in MSD (for scheduling only).
DO NOT order parts in MSD → Use:

https://commercial.lenovo.com/us/en/partsordering


WO must clearly state:

Parts will be shipped directly to customer site from another platform.


Parts must always be delivered to:

PARIS ITX – Interxion PAR3 




3. Access & Support

First-time users need Operations Leader (Nader Labib) for:

Portal access
Training


Lenovo escalation/contact:

Zebada Leung (lzebada@lenovo.com / +852 6077 7417)


Zebada supports RTS & RMC for access issues. 


4. Critical Rules

Disks must stay with the customer.
SSR must take photos of serial numbers:

Old disk
New disk


Photos must be sent to Zebada.
Machines are not visible in GLOSSE (confidential / restricted hyperscale servers). 


5. Preferred SSRs

Leang Houy Kech
Pierre Fernandes

1. Communication & Contacts

OOH team must contact the France standby agent to handle the case.
All communication must be in English via email:

📧 lenovosoge@lenovo.com


Access authorization requests:

Graham Boxall (gboxall@lenovo.com)
Weronika Grzeda (wgrzeda@lenovo.com)


Note: 24h access is always requested by Lenovo


2. Troubleshooting & Work Orders

Lenovo onsite team performs L1 troubleshooting.
RTS can create a work order without logs if Lenovo requests a specific FRU.
Do NOT recommend firmware updates unless requested by L3/PE. 


3. Site & Access Rules

Location:

Interxion PAR 7
Room: 1.R111.0 / Cage: 1.R111.D
1-3 rue Rateau, 93120 La Courneuve, France


Control Center must NOT ask for server location. 

4. Parts Handling & Delivery

SSR must collect parts from PUDO (direct delivery = exception only).

Ordering Rules

Before 4 PM (cut-off):

Use NBD → delivery to AccPt (La Courneuve)


After cut-off / weekends / holidays:

Use SBD → delivery to FSL (Rungis)


Sev1 cases (production down):

Always SBD → FSL Rungis

6. SLA

All machines covered under 24x7 / 4-hour SLA

1. Handover (HO): Ensure sending HO of all your cases (MAHI / CSP in case of growth).
2. Case Ownership: Do not reassign case ownership to any team member who is currently on annual leave.
3. Growth Case Tracking: All growth case subjects must include the MSD reference number, and vice versa, to improve tracking and visibility.
4. MAHI Case Titles: Please ensure MAHI case titles are properly updated and clearly reflect the case context.
5. Customer Satisfaction (PCC Stage): If you identify any signs of customer dissatisfaction during PCC, do not close the case before consulting with Operation leader.

SBD - https://splus.ups.com/cfw/trackOrder.action
